approval_scope: Build a 150-second cinematic proof before applying the grammar to the full film.
visual_grammar: Alternate official evidence and source-derived graphics with semantically relevant licensed footage.
Use full-screen emphasis cards only at deliberate narration pauses.
Never present contextual footage as literal proof of a named event, company, laboratory, or government.
Keep source labels visible on every evidence scene and preserve the controlled-test limitation.
sound_grammar: Use one continuously licensed cinematic bed with distinct energy states.
Duck music under speech but let it rise during editorial pauses.
Use only sparse original synthesized impacts, blooms, and ticks tied to editorial events.
Do not add random whooshes, glitch effects, meme sounds, or full-audio silence.

full_film_sections.visual_balance: Cinematic context first, then safety frameworks and a present-tense emphasis beat.
Test-condition footage intercut with official screenshots, figures, and a strong limitation beat.
Markets, institutions, and competitive movement alternate with the AI Index evidence.
Operational cybersecurity and lab context support — but never substitute for — attributed source evidence.
Human work and infrastructure footage break up labour and competition documents.
Institutional footage, legal text, and restrained power motifs share the screen rhythm.
A clear two-sided visual grammar holds openness and centralized control in tension.
Audits, incident response, compute governance, and international verification gain constructive motion.
Reduce density, lengthen shots, return to people, and allow the music to decay after the final line.

full_narration_dependency.reason: The narration after the validated 114.2-second prefix predates the current approved script.
full_narration_dependency.recording_rule: Generate one continuous narration from voice/voice_script.txt; do not assemble the stale remaining segments.
